prompt: I want to understand the range of possible Year 3 Gross Profit outcomes. Revenue growth could be anywhere from 5% to 15% (uniform distribution) and COGS efficiency could be 0% to 2% improvement per year (also uniform). Run a Monte Carlo simulation with 100 iterations and give me the distribution.
assertions: Reports distribution statistics (mean ~$5.07M, median, P10, P90, std dev)
Samples correctly from uniform(0.05, 0.15) for growth and uniform(0.00, 0.02) for COGS efficiency
Uses the write-recalculate-read pattern for each iteration (not internal Python eval)
Restores the model to base case values after simulation
The mean (~$5.07M) is close to the base case ($5.08M), confirming calibration
Presents results in a format that supports decision-making
